currentObjective: Build theme-aware interactive question scenes for early years, using child theme and favorite tags to make questions feel world-specific and joyful.

product.problemStatement: Parents need a dashboard that clearly shows topic-level strengths and weaknesses, warns when a child is struggling, keeps motivation high with a visible streak, and surfaces a weekly digest in-app instead of relying on email.
product.user: Parent managing multiple children, plus the child using the learning and reward flows.
useCases: Parent opens Progress tab and sees topic-level performance pie charts
Parent gets a smart alert when the child is struggling on a topic
Child maintains a streak and sees it on the dashboard
Parent reads an in-app weekly digest without waiting for email
nfrs: Reuse the existing progress model and streak fields where possible
Keep the alert logic conservative so it does not spam parents
Keep the UI compact and responsive on laptop, tablet, and mobile
Do not break existing progress, reward, or diagnostic flows
acceptanceCriteria: Progress tab shows a subject pie chart and topic drill-down for the selected child
Needs-help alert appears for topics where recent accuracy falls below 50%
Child streak is visible and continues to update from sessions
Weekly in-app digest shows questions, accuracy, streak, points, and recent sessions
antiScope: Do NOT change the diagnostic API contract
Do NOT remove the existing reward or question-report flows
Do NOT break the existing weekly email cron route

codexTasks: Render a focused progress dashboard with subject pie charts and topic drill-down
Show needs-help alerts and weekly digest summary in-app
Keep streak and existing progress/reward/diagnostic flows intact
claudeTasks: Test the new progress topics API and subject pie chart UI
Test the needs-help alert logic and weekly digest endpoint
Verify streak badges and existing dashboard sections still work
Check responsiveness on laptop and tablet layouts
Add regression coverage for subscription checkout and portal flows
Prove progress scores remain per-subject and untouched subjects stay at zero

mergePolicy: No merge without explicit user approval.

workstreams.goal: Implement the repo-native coordination layer and dashboard tabs foundation.
chunks: Add tab routing and section state
Split dashboard into Students, Progress, Rewards, and Account sections
Add diagnostic badges and preserve existing actions
Smoke test and hand off to Claude

workstreams.goal: Build a dedicated diagnostic page for the existing diagnostic API and connect the dashboard badge into it.
chunks: Submit 5 answers to the existing diagnostic endpoint
Hand off to Claude for test coverage

workstreams.goal: Add a progress summary API and render subject/session charts on the dashboard progress tab.
chunks: Render subject accuracy cards and session history

workstreams.goal: Let parents save child topic preferences and use those preferences in question selection.
chunks: Filter question selection by preferences when available

workstreams.goal: Add an avatar/theme reward shop and allow point-based redemption.
chunks: Render shop items in rewards page
Keep the gift card catalog working

workstreams.goal: Send weekly parent email summaries for active/trial subscriptions.
Build theme-aware interactive question scenes for early years, so counting and shape questions feel world-specific, joyful, and aligned with the childs favorite topics.
chunks: TapCountStage.tsx - tappable counting scene with world/favorite-tag context
DotJoinStage.tsx - SVG dot-join with pointer tracking and shape close detection
Update QuestionVisualStage.tsx dispatcher (early years -> interactive, others -> static)
tsc --noEmit passes and theme-context tests are green
claudeTasks: Test the theme-aware scene mapping for soccer, space, jungle, ocean, fantasy, and unicorn worlds
Verify favorite topic tags can steer the world selection and child context
Run existing visual and interactive regression suites to confirm no breakage
Check that the question screen still feels like the childs chosen world across dashboard and learn flow

coordinationRules: Read the shared task, ownership, status, handoff, and message files before starting new work.
Poll the other agent every 5 seconds while actively working.
Update your own status every 30-60 seconds and on every blocker change.
Keep write scopes disjoint unless the task explicitly says otherwise.
Product owns requirements quality, use cases, and non-functional expectations before implementation starts.
